description: Agile threat modeling as code -- define your architecture in YAML, auto-generate risk reports, data flow diagrams, and security recommendations. Integrates into CI/CD pipelines.

name: OWASP pytm
description: Pythonic framework for threat modeling -- define your system architecture in Python code, automatically generate data flow diagrams, sequence diagrams, and threat lists.

name: Deciduous Attack Tree Builder
description: Free browser-based visual attack tree builder. Create and share attack trees with a simple text-based syntax that renders as interactive diagrams. No installation required.

name: IriusRisk Community Edition
description: Free tier of the enterprise threat modeling platform with questionnaire-driven threat identification, component-based modeling, and countermeasure recommendations.

name: CAIRIS
description: Open-source platform for security, usability, and requirements engineering with built-in threat modeling, attack tree generation, and risk analysis capabilities.

name: Threat Modeling Resources (Adam Shostack)
description: Free companion resources from the author of 'Threat Modeling: Designing for Security,' including presentations, worksheets, and links to the Elevation of Privilege card game.

name: Elevation of Privilege Card Game
description: Gamified threat modeling through a card game that teaches STRIDE categories via collaborative play. Teams draw cards describing threats and discuss whether they apply to the system under review.

name: EFF Threat Modeling Guide (Your Security Plan)
description: EFF's beginner-friendly introduction to threat modeling for individuals. Teaches how to identify assets, adversaries, threats, and capabilities as the foundation for a personal security plan.

name: HHS 405(d) Health Industry Cybersecurity Practices (HICP)
description: Free, voluntary cybersecurity practices for the healthcare industry developed by HHS and industry experts. Includes tailored guidance for small, medium, and large organizations with specific threat mitigations.

name: NIST SP 800-66 Rev. 2: HIPAA Security Rule Implementation Guide
description: NIST's definitive guide for implementing the HIPAA Security Rule. Maps HIPAA requirements to NIST Cybersecurity Framework controls with practical implementation guidance for healthcare organizations.

name: HITRUST CSF Overview
description: Overview of the HITRUST Common Security Framework, the most widely adopted security and privacy framework in the U.S. healthcare industry. Integrates HIPAA, NIST, ISO, PCI, and other standards into a single certifiable framework.

name: HHS Cybersecurity Performance Goals (CPGs) for Healthcare
description: Sector-specific cybersecurity performance goals for healthcare and public health organizations. Defines essential and enhanced goals across access control, asset management, email security, incident response, and more.

name: FDA Premarket Cybersecurity Guidance for Medical Devices

name: NIST Cybersecurity Framework Healthcare Profile
description: NIST CSF quick-start resources with healthcare sector-specific guidance. Maps the Framework's core functions to healthcare-relevant controls, supporting HIPAA compliance and health sector risk management.

name: HIPAA Security Rule Crosswalk to NIST CSF
description: Official HHS crosswalk mapping HIPAA Security Rule requirements to NIST Cybersecurity Framework subcategories. Essential for organizations aligning healthcare compliance with broader cybersecurity standards.

name: HHS HC3 – Health Sector Cybersecurity Coordination Center
description: HHS's cybersecurity threat intelligence center for the healthcare sector. Publishes analyst notes, threat briefings, vulnerability bulletins, and sector-specific alerts on ransomware, APTs, and emerging threats.

name: Health-ISAC
description: The healthcare sector's Information Sharing and Analysis Center. Provides threat intelligence sharing, vulnerability alerts, best practice guides, and a community of healthcare security professionals.

name: CISA Healthcare and Public Health Sector Resources
description: CISA's dedicated resource hub for healthcare and public health critical infrastructure. Includes sector risk assessments, mitigation guides, incident response support, and vulnerability advisories specific to healthcare.

name: MITRE Medical Device Cybersecurity Playbook
description: MITRE's regional incident preparedness and response playbook for medical device cybersecurity. Covers response planning, stakeholder coordination, and recovery procedures for healthcare delivery organizations.

name: CISA Healthcare and Public Health Mitigation Guide
description: Free cybersecurity toolkit for healthcare organizations from CISA. Includes risk assessment templates, incident response checklists, staff training materials, and implementation guides tailored to healthcare environments.

name: (ISC)² HCISPP Overview and Study Resources
description: Overview of the HealthCare Information Security and Privacy Practitioner certification. Includes the exam outline, domain summaries, and free study resources for professionals managing healthcare data security.

description: Archived talks from SANS Health Care Cybersecurity Summits. Features expert presentations on healthcare threat landscape, medical device security, incident response in hospitals, and regulatory compliance strategies.

description: Grassroots cybersecurity initiative advocating for safety and security in medical devices and healthcare systems. The Hippocratic Oath for Connected Medical Devices outlines five principles for patient safety.

name: HL7 FHIR Security and Privacy Module
description: Security guidance for the HL7 FHIR healthcare interoperability standard. Covers authentication, authorization, audit logging, access control, digital signatures, and encryption for healthcare data exchange APIs.

name: CISA Medical Device Cybersecurity Advisories
description: CISA's advisories database filtered for medical device vulnerabilities. Includes ICS-CERT alerts, vulnerability disclosures, and mitigation guidance for healthcare equipment from major manufacturers.

name: NTIA/CISA Healthcare SBOM Proof of Concept
description: CISA's Software Bill of Materials initiative with healthcare as a key use case. Includes SBOM format guidance, healthcare PoC results, tooling recommendations, and frameworks for medical device supply chain transparency.

name: HC3 Analyst Notes and Threat Briefs
description: Library of HC3 publications including analyst notes on ransomware groups targeting healthcare, vulnerability bulletins for medical systems, sector alerts, and white papers on emerging threats to the health sector.

name: Archimedes Center for Healthcare and Device Security
description: Academic research center focused on healthcare cybersecurity and medical device security. Publishes research on clinical system vulnerabilities, implantable device security, and healthcare network defense.

name: HHS HIPAA Breach Portal (Wall of Shame)
description: HHS Office for Civil Rights breach reporting portal listing all reported healthcare data breaches affecting 500+ individuals. Invaluable for studying breach trends, attack vectors, and regulatory enforcement patterns in healthcare.

name: Cynerio Healthcare Cybersecurity Research
description: Healthcare IoT security vendor research covering medical device vulnerability trends, clinical network attack patterns, and state of healthcare cybersecurity reports. Free research reports available.

name: MedCrypt Medical Device Security
description: Medical device cybersecurity company providing free resources on FDA compliance, embedded device encryption, SBOM management, and secure development practices for healthcare technology manufacturers.

name: Harvard CS50: Introduction to Computer Science
description: Harvard's flagship CS course covering C, Python, SQL, web programming, data structures, and algorithms. Entirely free with optional verified certificate. The foundation for understanding systems that security professionals protect.

name: Stanford CS161: Computer Security
description: Stanford's computer security course with free lecture recordings, slides, and textbook. Covers memory safety, x86 assembly, cryptography, web security, and network security from a CS perspective.

name: A Graduate Course in Applied Cryptography (Boneh & Shoup)
description: Free textbook by Dan Boneh and Victor Shoup covering the mathematical foundations of modern cryptography. The definitive academic reference for applied cryptographic theory.

name: Stanford CS110: Principles of Computer Systems
description: Archived Stanford course with lecture videos covering filesystems, multiprocessing, multithreading, and networking. Essential systems programming knowledge for security engineers.

name: Nand2Tetris: Building a Modern Computer from First Principles

name: Operating Systems: Three Easy Pieces (OSTEP)
description: Free online textbook covering virtualization, concurrency, and persistence in operating systems. Covers processes, scheduling, memory management, paging, file systems, and security -- critical for exploit development and defense.

name: LiveOverflow (YouTube)

name: Beej's Guide to Network Programming

name: Khan Academy: Algorithms
description: Free interactive course on algorithms: binary search, sorting, recursion, graph algorithms, and asymptotic analysis (Big-O notation). Foundational CS knowledge for security professionals.

name: SEI CERT Secure Coding Standards
description: Free wiki of secure coding rules for C, C++, Java, Perl, and Android developed by 1,900+ contributors. The authoritative reference for writing vulnerability-free code, maintained by CMU's CERT division.

name: CERT/CC Vulnerability Notes Database
description: Searchable database of 3,500+ vulnerability notes covering 2,300+ vendors. Each note includes technical details, impact assessment, remediation guidance, and affected vendor information.

name: CERT Guide to Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure
description: Comprehensive guide to the coordinated vulnerability disclosure process. Covers discovery, reporting, coordination, and publication phases with ethical and legal considerations.

name: SSVC: Stakeholder-Specific Vulnerability Categorization
description: Decision-tree based vulnerability prioritization framework developed by CMU CERT and adopted by CISA. Alternative to CVSS that considers exploitation status, mission impact, and exposure.

name: Common Sense Guide to Mitigating Insider Threats (6th Ed.)
description: CMU CERT's definitive guide with 22 practices for insider threat mitigation, drawn from real-world case studies. Covers organizational, HR, technical, and legal countermeasures.

name: Cilium: eBPF-based Kubernetes Networking and Security
description: eBPF-powered CNI plugin for Kubernetes providing networking, observability, and security enforcement. Implements network policies, transparent encryption, and runtime security at the kernel level.

name: Istio Service Mesh
description: Service mesh providing mTLS, traffic management, and observability for Kubernetes microservices. Enables zero-trust networking with automatic encryption between pods.

name: Calico: Kubernetes Network Policy and Security
description: Open-source Kubernetes networking and network security solution. Provides fine-grained network policies, encryption in transit, and compliance reporting for containerized workloads.

name: K3s: Lightweight Kubernetes

name: Karpenter: Kubernetes Node Autoscaling
description: Open-source Kubernetes node provisioner that launches right-sized compute resources in response to pending pods. Key for cost optimization and security group enforcement in cloud-native environments.

name: Crossplane: Infrastructure as Code for Kubernetes
description: Open-source Kubernetes add-on that enables provisioning and managing cloud infrastructure using Kubernetes-native APIs. Compose infrastructure from multiple providers with policy guardrails.

name: OpenTofu

name: vCluster: Virtual Kubernetes Clusters

name: Azure Well-Architected Framework

description: Google Cloud's best practices for system design covering security, reliability, cost optimization, and operational excellence. Security section covers identity, encryption, network security, and logging.

description: Official AWS guide to securing S3 buckets: blocking public access, using bucket policies, enabling encryption, access logging, versioning, Object Lock, and S3 Access Points.

name: AWS EKS Best Practices Guide
description: Comprehensive security, networking, reliability, and cost optimization best practices for Amazon EKS. Covers pod security, IAM roles for service accounts, network policies, and secrets management.

name: IANA Service Name and Port Number Registry
description: The authoritative registry of TCP/UDP port number assignments maintained by IANA. Essential reference for firewall rules, network analysis, and understanding service-to-port mappings.

name: IETF RFC Index

name: Practical Networking: Networking Fundamentals
description: Free article and video series covering OSI model, TCP/IP, subnetting, routing, switching, ARP, NAT, and VLAN fundamentals. Clear visual explanations with packet-level detail.

description: Official RHEL 9 documentation covering installation, security hardening, SELinux, identity management, networking, storage, and system administration. Free to access.

name: Ubuntu Server Guide

name: Windows Server Documentation
description: Official Microsoft Windows Server documentation covering Active Directory, Group Policy, WSUS, Hyper-V, failover clustering, storage, and security baselines.

name: CIS Benchmarks

name: GSMA 5G Security Resources
description: GSMA's telecom security hub covering 5G security, SIM security, fraud prevention, and mobile network security guidelines. Includes the GSMA Security Assurance Scheme (NESAS) documentation.

description: CISA's resources for the Communications critical infrastructure sector. Covers telecom resilience, SS7 vulnerabilities, supply chain risks, and emergency communications security.

name: Martin Fowler's Architecture Guide
description: Free articles on software architecture including microservices, event-driven architecture, domain-driven design, continuous delivery, and the architectural trade-offs that impact security.

name: TOGAF Standard (Open Group)

name: SABSA Security Architecture
description: Overview of the Sherwood Applied Business Security Architecture framework for developing security architectures driven by business requirements. The leading security-specific architecture methodology.

name: NIST SP 800-160 Vol. 1: Systems Security Engineering
description: NIST's guide for engineering trustworthy secure systems. Integrates security into every phase of the system development lifecycle with architecture-level security design principles.

name: Mermaid: Diagrams as Code
description: Open-source JavaScript-based diagramming tool that renders text definitions into flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, state diagrams, and architecture diagrams. Native in GitHub, GitLab, and Notion.

name: PTES: Penetration Testing Execution Standard
description: Industry standard defining the seven phases of a penetration test: pre-engagement, intelligence gathering, threat modeling, vulnerability analysis, exploitation, post-exploitation, and reporting.

name: OWASP Testing Guide v4.2
description: Version 4.2 of the OWASP Testing Guide with 91 specific test procedures organized across 11 categories. The most widely referenced web application penetration testing methodology.

name: PayloadsAllTheThings
description: Comprehensive list of payloads and bypass techniques for web application security. Covers SQL injection, XSS, SSRF, XXE, directory traversal, command injection, and dozens more attack categories.

name: SecLists: Security Tester's Companion
description: Collection of wordlists for security testing: usernames, passwords, URLs, fuzzing payloads, web shells, and more. The standard companion for Burp Suite, ffuf, and other security tools.

name: PostgreSQL Documentation
description: Official PostgreSQL documentation including security chapters on authentication, roles, row-level security, encryption, and SSL/TLS configuration. The most widely used open-source relational database.

name: OWASP Database Security Cheat Sheet
description: Quick-reference guide for securing databases: hardening, access control, parameterized queries, encryption at rest and in transit, auditing, and backup security across SQL and NoSQL systems.

description: Static analysis tool for Terraform code detecting security misconfigurations. Checks for unencrypted resources, overly permissive IAM, public access, and insecure defaults across AWS, Azure, and GCP.

name: KICS: Keeping Infrastructure as Code Secure

description: NIST's guide for conducting risk assessments. Covers threat identification, vulnerability analysis, likelihood determination, impact analysis, and risk determination with qualitative and semi-quantitative approaches.

name: RiskLens FAIR Fundamentals Training
description: Free resources for learning the FAIR quantitative risk analysis model including webinars, white papers, and case studies on translating cyber risk into financial terms for executive communication.

name: NIST SP 800-60: Guide to Mapping Information Types
description: NIST guide for mapping information types to security categories. Essential for data classification programs -- provides the taxonomy and methodology for categorizing federal information systems.

name: NIST SP 800-86: Guide to Integrating Forensic Techniques
description: NIST guide for incorporating forensic techniques into incident response. Covers data collection from files, OS, network traffic, and applications with chain of custody procedures and legal considerations.

name: SANS Digital Forensics and Incident Response Blog
description: SANS DFIR blog with case studies, tool tutorials, memory forensics techniques, and investigation walkthroughs from certified forensic examiners.

description: Kali Linux forensics tool catalog including Autopsy, Binwalk, bulk_extractor, dc3dd, Foremost, Galleta, hashdeep, and volatility. Each tool page includes usage documentation and examples.

description: CISA's physical security resources including active shooter preparedness, bomb threat guidance, facility security, mail screening, and physical security assessment checklists.

description: NIST guidelines for using Personal Identity Verification (PIV) credentials for physical access control systems. Covers authentication mechanisms, PACS architecture, and federal facility integration.

name: The Open Organisation of Lockpickers (TOOOL)
description: Nonprofit organization providing education on physical security and lock vulnerabilities. Resources include lockpicking tutorials, physical security assessment techniques, and educational presentations at DEF CON.

name: NIST SP 800-183: Networks of Things (IoT/Aerospace)
description: NIST framework for understanding networks of things including aerospace, satellite, and aviation systems. Covers composition patterns, trust relationships, and security considerations for cyber-physical systems.

name: Aviation ISAC Resources
description: Aviation sector Information Sharing and Analysis Center providing cybersecurity threat intelligence, best practices, and incident coordination for airlines, airports, and aerospace manufacturers.

name: Nevada Gaming Control Board MICS
description: Minimum Internal Control Standards for Nevada gaming operations covering IT security, surveillance, access control, and cybersecurity requirements for casinos and gaming technology.

name: Adversarial Robustness Toolbox (ART)
description: Python library for ML security covering adversarial attacks, defenses, robustness verification, and certification. Supports TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, and scikit-learn.

name: UiPath Academy (Free RPA Courses)
